summary.intent: Implement the framework-agnostic SegmentGroup component (issue #232) in ars-components, covering state machine, ARIA attributes, roving tabindex, hidden input, and tests — without Leptos/Dioxus adapters.
summary.outcome: Fully implemented SegmentGroup core (~1530 lines), all test suites passing (unit, snapshot, spec-conformance, proptest), spec corrected across three files, committed to branch codex/issue-232-segment-group-core, and issue moved to In Progress on the project board.

repo: Components follow radio_group/toggle_group conventions: controlled mode emits Effect::ValueChange without mutating internal value; uncontrolled mode mutates then emits.
Focus is handled as a logical Effect::FocusItem — no ars-dom dependency; adapters own the actual DOM focus via mounted element handles.
Items register/unregister dynamically via RegisterItem/UnregisterItem events; roving tabindex anchor resolves at render time from registered state.
Spec is layered across three files: spec/components/ (agnostic core), spec/leptos-components/, spec/dioxus-components/ — all three must be kept consistent.
cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject fails globally on pre-existing unrelated unreferenced snapshots in other components; only the module-filtered form is reliable during new component work.
cargo xtask spec validate is the authoritative spec consistency gate and must pass before the diff is considered complete.
GitHub project board item status is updated via gh project item-edit with --field-id and --single-select-option-id; gh project item-list --query is not supported.

code.finding: New file: full SegmentGroup implementation including State/Event/Props/Context/Effect/Part types, ConnectApi with all attr methods and event handlers, controlled/uncontrolled logic, roving tabindex, hidden input attrs, item registration, reset handling, and inline unit/snapshot tests.
Added three SegmentGroup proptest invariants: only enabled items become roving anchors, selected value is at most one key, and ConnectApi::part_attrs equals the corresponding explicit attr method.
Added spec-conformance anatomy coverage for all five SegmentGroup parts: Root, Item, ItemText, Indicator, HiddenInput.
Parity appendix had stale Ark-style names (Label, ItemControl, ItemHiddenInput, ItemIndicator) and 'via Bindable' value-change wording — corrected to match the implemented five-part core.

workflow: TDD order: write all failing tests first to get a red baseline, then implement until green — prevents retrofitting tests to implementation details.

Post-implementation audit (coverage + spec drift scan) is a mandatory gate: it surfaced real untested branches (registration, reset, disabled guards) and three spec inconsistencies that would have misled adapter implementors.
cargo llvm-cov output covers the entire crate; AWK filtering on the module path is required to isolate coverage lines for a single new component.
Snapshots generated with INSTA_UPDATE=always on a targeted test, then accepted — avoids accepting unrelated pending snapshots.
Detached HEAD worktrees require git switch -c before commit; check HEAD state before assuming a branch exists.
Use cargo fmt --all before running snapshot tests to avoid diff noise and potential format-induced snapshot mismatches.

friction: cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject blocked by pre-existing unrelated unreferenced snapshots in other components — the global flag cannot be used safely until those are cleaned up.
Detached HEAD state in the worktree required an explicit git switch -c before commit; not obvious from git status -sb output.
gh project item-list --query flag is not supported; finding the correct item ID required fetching all 500 items and grepping manually, then separately fetching field IDs.
Spec had contradictory anatomy sections — §1.6 five-part core vs. a later parity appendix using Ark-style six-part naming — requiring two separate audit passes to fully resolve.
cargo llvm-cov produces a monolithic text report; no built-in way to scope to one module, requiring AWK post-processing across 120k+ line output.
open_items: Leptos adapter for SegmentGroup not implemented — explicitly out of scope for #232, will require a follow-on issue.
Dioxus adapter for SegmentGroup not implemented — same deferral as Leptos.
Pre-existing unreferenced insta snapshots in other ars-components modules block the global --unreferenced=reject gate and should be cleaned up in a separate pass.
Issue #232 not closed — intentionally deferred until PR is reviewed and merged.
PR push was requested at the end of the transcript but completion was not shown — needs confirmation that the push and PR creation succeeded.
